title: Cyclobalanopsis poilanei
taxonomy: Cyclobalanopsis poilanei is a species of plant in the family Fagaceae. This species is native to Vietnam. It belongs to the genus Cyclobalanopsis, which is a group of deciduous trees and shrubs. The Fagaceae family includes other species of oak and beech trees. Cyclobalanopsis poilanei is often found in mountainous regions, where it grows on rocky or mountainous terrain.
uses: Cyclobalanopsis poilanei, a species of oak tree, has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Vietnamese folk medicine. Its bark and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in treating various health conditions. intro: Cyclobalanopsis poilanei, also known as Polilana oak, is a species of deciduous tree in the family Fagaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
